Title: Evolution of pyrrolizidine alkaloid biosynthesis in Apocynaceae: revisiting the defence de-escalation hypothesis

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Parsonsia alboflavescens / Apocynaceae APSA clade species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant chemical defense; exploited/sequestered by specialist Danainae butterflies
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Often roots more than shoots in at least some Apocynaceae species
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: First committed/entry step of PA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an aminobutyl moiety to putrescine to form homospermidine, the first specific precursor step for PA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms pathway entry precursor required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first pathway-specific enzyme; key control point for presence/absence of PA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Parsonsia alboflavescens, HSS-encoding cDNA was amplified from shoot cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not detected in Asclepias syriaca shoot and bud transcriptomes; otherwise variable among sampled species
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome database mining: 1KP; Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; Phytometasyn; Asclepias syriaca genome and transcriptome assemblies; GenBank nucleotide sequences
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of intact hss, especially with the HSS-associated V/D motif, is associated with capacity for pyrrolizidine alkaloid biosynthesis; pseudogenization or motif loss is consistent with PA loss
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; enzyme activity assays with radiolabelled putrescine and eIF5A precursor substrate discrimination; phylogenetic analysis; ancestral sequence reconstruction; motif analysis; pseudogene verification by PCR/cloning/Sanger sequencing
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Parsonsia alboflavescens encodes a functional HSS with high activity using putrescine and no detectable DHS activity with eIF5A. 2. hss originated once in Apocynaceae early in APSA clade evolution. 3. hss occurs in all sampled PA-positive genera but also in some PA-negative or untested taxa. 4. Loss of the HSS-associated V/D motif and pseudogenization in some lineages are consistent with multiple independent losses of PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker or engineering entry point for PA production/elimination; disabling hss could reduce PA accumulation, whereas introducing functional hss alone may be insufficient because downstream PA-pathway steps are also required
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PA loss may alter defense against generalist herbivores while potentially reducing exploitation by PA-adapted specialists; intact hss does not guarantee PA accumulation due to downstream pathway or expression constraints
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The V/D motif appears necessary but not sufficient for full HSS function; some species lacking reported PAs still possess intact hss-like sequences, implying possible silencing, downstream pathway loss, organ-specific accumulation, or incomplete chemical sampling

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Not a PA-pathway enzyme; ancestral paralog of hss
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: eIF5A precursor protein; also putrescine in assay context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an aminobutyl moiety to the eIF5A precursor protein in hypusine biosynthesis; served here as the ancestral paralog from which HSS evolved
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies evolutionary background comparator rather than direct PA-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Parsonsia alboflavescens, DHS-encoding cDNA was amplified from root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly detected as essential housekeeping paralog in many sampled species, but not recovered in all due to sampling limitations
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome and genome database mining; GenBank; 1KP; Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; Phytometasyn
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the ancestral non-PA paralog used to infer hss origin and functional divergence; not itself a core determinant of PA accumulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; enzyme assays with recombinant eIF5A precursor and putrescine; phylogenetic comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Parsonsia alboflavescens contains a functional DHS with high activity toward eIF5A and putrescine. 2. dhs is the ancestral paralog from which hss evolved by duplication in the APSA clade. 3. The DHS-like I/N motif contrasts with the HSS-associated V/D motif and helps predict functional divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value for nutrient/metabolite engineering because DHS is essential and not PA-specific
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating dhs would likely disrupt primary cellular function because it is essential and not specialized for PA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a functionally characterized comparator central to establishing hss evolution, but it is not the pathway-specific PA enzyme
